component.name: DropdownMenu
component.description: A trigger that opens a Menu popover of actions or options. Owns the open/close state, outside-click and Escape dismissal, and focus return; the Menu surface it wraps holds the rows (actions, checkboxes, radios, groups, separators).
component.note: Trigger is styled like the neutral Button (sharp rect, white fill, neutral.300 border, forest tint on hover). Composes Menu — items/spacing/maxHeight/menuLabel pass straight through. While open the trigger takes the forest.50 selected tint and the chevron flips up. Synced to Figma '❖ Dropdown menu'.

triggerStates.hover: colors.background.primary.weak.hovered (forest.100) fill, forest border, colors.text.primary.strong (forest.700) text
triggerStates.press: colors.background.primary.weak.pressed (forest.200) fill, forest border, forest.700 text
triggerStates.open: colors.background.primary.weak (forest.50) selected tint, colors.border.primary.strong (forest.700) border, forest.700 text; chevron rotates 180deg

popover.position: absolute, top calc(100% + spacing.2xs) — 4px below the trigger

tokens.menu: Surface tokens are owned by the nested Menu component (forest.50 / forest.700 selected rows, neutral row text, 40px / 32px row heights).

useCases: Expose a set of actions behind a labelled button (e.g. 'Choices', 'Sort by')
An overflow / 'more' menu behind an icon trigger (more-horizontal)
A filter or multi-select control where checkbox rows stay open while toggling
A single-choice option list using radio rows that closes on pick
Row-level or toolbar actions in Athena Tools surfaces

commonPatterns.name: Action menu
commonPatterns.description: A labelled button opening a short list of commands that close the menu on pick.
commonPatterns.jsx: <DropdownMenu triggerLabel='Actions' items={[{ id: 'edit', label: 'Edit' }, { id: 'dup', label: 'Duplicate' }, { type: 'separator' }, { id: 'del', label: 'Delete' }]} onSelect={(id) => run(id)} />

commonPatterns.name: Icon overflow menu
commonPatterns.description: Icon-only trigger for a row or toolbar overflow menu; aria-label gives it an accessible name and bottom-end keeps it on screen.
commonPatterns.jsx: <DropdownMenu triggerType='icon' triggerIcon='more-horizontal' aria-label='More actions' placement='bottom-end' items={items} />

commonPatterns.name: Multi-select filter
commonPatterns.description: Checkbox rows keep the menu open so several can be toggled before dismissing; selection is driven by isSelected on each row.
commonPatterns.jsx: <DropdownMenu triggerLabel='Filter' items={topics.map((t) => ({ id: t.id, type: 'checkbox', label: t.name, isSelected: t.on }))} onSelect={(id) => toggle(id)} />

commonPatterns.name: Controlled open state
commonPatterns.description: Drive open/close from the parent via isOpen + onOpenChange (e.g. to coordinate with other UI).

antiPatterns.scenario: Using DropdownMenu for primary page navigation
antiPatterns.reason: It is a transient action/option popover, not a persistent wayfinding affordance; hiding top-level destinations behind a trigger hurts discoverability.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Tabs or plain links for primary navigation.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using DropdownMenu as a form select where a labelled value is validated
antiPatterns.reason: It has no associated form value, label, or validation surface, so submit/validation flows can't see the chosen option.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a labelled native/DS select or a RadioGroup inside the form.

antiPatterns.scenario: An icon trigger with no aria-label
antiPatterns.reason: An icon-only button has no visible text, so it exposes no accessible name to screen readers.

antiPatterns.scenario: Setting closeOnSelect={true} on a multi-select checkbox menu
antiPatterns.reason: Forcing close after every checkbox toggle breaks the multi-select flow the default behavior is built for.
antiPatterns.alternative: Leave closeOnSelect unset — checkbox rows keep the menu open by default; only action/radio rows close.

slots.triggerLabel: Text for a button trigger (default 'Select'); ignored for icon triggers
slots.triggerIcon: Icon for an icon trigger (default 'more-horizontal'); ignored for button triggers
slots.items: MenuNode[] passed straight through to the nested Menu — action / checkbox / radio rows, groups, and separators

parentConstraints: Any inline-block layout — the wrapper is position:relative and the popover absolutely positioned beneath it

interactions.hover: trigger → forest.100 fill, forest border, forest.700 text
interactions.press: trigger → forest.200 fill, forest border, forest.700 text
interactions.open: trigger takes the forest.50 selected tint with forest.700 border/text; chevron flips up (rotate 180); popover renders 4px below, z-index 20, aligned by placement
interactions.openFromTrigger: Click toggles; ArrowDown / Enter / Space on the trigger opens and focuses the first enabled row
interactions.dismiss: Outside mousedown closes; Escape closes and returns focus to the trigger; Tab closes without stealing focus back
interactions.select: closeOnSelect defaults true EXCEPT a 'checkbox' row keeps the menu open (multi-select); action and radio rows close. Override per-instance with closeOnSelect.

behavior.controlled: Pass isOpen + onOpenChange for controlled; omit isOpen and pass defaultOpen for uncontrolled.
behavior.responsive: Wrapper is inline-block; popover is absolutely positioned via plain CSS (no floating-ui), so it does not auto-flip — pick placement ('bottom-start' / 'bottom-end') to keep it on screen.

accessibility.role: button trigger with aria-haspopup='menu'; the popover surface is the Menu (role='menu')
accessibility.trigger: aria-haspopup='menu' always; aria-expanded reflects open; aria-controls points at the menu id only while open; icon trigger requires aria-label for an accessible name
accessibility.focusManagement: Opening focuses the first enabled row (autoFocusFirst on Menu); Escape and outside-Tab close, and Escape returns focus to the trigger
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Trigger: ArrowDown / Enter / Space open and focus the first row. Menu: ArrowUp/Down, Home/End roving-tabindex navigation, Enter/Space select, Escape closes, Tab closes.
accessibility.screenReader: Trigger announces its expanded/collapsed state and that it controls a menu; rows announce as menuitem / menuitemcheckbox / menuitemradio with aria-checked from the Menu.
accessibility.contrastNote: neutral.600 trigger text on white and forest.700 text on forest.50 (open) both meet AA.

athenaConstraints.triggerFill: white default; forest.50 selected tint when open (single brand accent)
athenaConstraints.checkboxKeepsOpen: checkbox rows stay open; action/radio rows close

figmaDivergences.figma: explicit state property (default / hover / press / focus / open)
figmaDivergences.implementation: real CSS pseudo-states (:hover, :active, :focus-visible) and a state-driven `open` class — no `state` prop
figmaDivergences.reason: Matches Button; interaction states are driven by the browser and open state rather than a prop.

figmaDivergences.implementation: plain CSS absolute positioning (top: 100% + 4px, left/right: 0) with no floating-ui dependency
figmaDivergences.reason: Lightweight; placement is chosen explicitly via the placement prop and the popover does not auto-flip or collision-detect.

aiHints.context: Reach for DropdownMenu to hang a list of actions or options off a trigger — a labelled button or an icon-only 'more' button. It composes Menu, so build the rows as MenuNode[] (action / checkbox / radio / group / separator). Use checkbox rows for multi-select (the menu stays open); use it for transient actions, not primary navigation or validated form values.
neverUseWhen: It is primary page navigation — use Tabs or links
It must hold a validated form value — use a labelled select or RadioGroup
The trigger is icon-only and you omit aria-label — it has no accessible name
You force closeOnSelect on a checkbox (multi-select) menu — let checkbox rows keep it open
